title: IT Service Catalog and Request Management System

prd.oneLiner: Connect service catalogs, request forms, approvals, fulfillment work, SLA, and demand analysis.
prd.goal: Raise self-service use of standard IT service requests to 70% or more.
prd.whyNow: IT services and request processes are spread across documents and email, so employees do not know what or how to request.
prd.problem: IT services and request processes are spread across documents and email, so employees do not know what or how to request.
prd.solution: Connect service catalogs, request forms, approvals, fulfillment work, SLA, and demand analysis.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: Employee requesting an IT service
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: IT services and request processes are spread across documents and email, so employees do not know what or how to request.

targets.name: IT service delivery coordinator
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: IT services and request processes are spread across documents and email, so employees do not know what or how to request.

scenarios.text: Employee requesting an IT service completes service-catalog search and request submission and checks progress.
IT service delivery coordinator reviews service demand, fulfillment time, sla, and automation-rate reporting.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Raise self-service use of standard IT service requests to 70% or more.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of completion history against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of registered requests connected to operations reporting

inScope: Service, request, approval, and fulfillment-status lookup
Request triage, approval, fulfillment, SLA, and completion confirmation
Service demand, fulfillment time, SLA, and automation-rate reporting
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
